Source_Species.reason: PhUGT708A43 is described as from bamboo and specifically from Phyllostachys heterocycla in Data availability.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper refers to Moso bamboo as Phyllostachys heterocycla cv. Pubescens and deposits PhUGT708A43 from Phyllostachys bamboo.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper defines Moso bamboo as Phyllostachys heterocycla cv. Pubescens (synonym: P. edulis).

Applied_Species.reason: PhUGT708A43 was expressed and used in engineered E. coli strains.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: E. coli is explicitly Escherichia coli in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state constructs were transformed into E. coli BL21(DE3).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PhUGT708A43 is a UGT708 family enzyme and characterized as a C-glycosyltransferase.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper repeatedly describes bamboo clade A1 enzymes including PhUGT708A43 as C-glycosyltransferases.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Assays used 2-hydroxynaringenin, phloretin, and UDP-glucose; PhUGT708A43 showed activity toward these acceptors with UDP-Glc donor.

Primary_Product.reason: Products included C-glucosylated 2-hydroxynaringenin intermediates, nothofagin from phloretin, and Vit/Isovit after dehydration; orientin/isoorientin were produced in full pathways using this CGT.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This matches Fig. 1 and Results: clade A1 CGTs transfer glucose from UDP-Glc to 2-hydroxylated intermediates or phloretin via C-glycosylation.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The entire study places PhUGT708A43 in flavone C-glycoside biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: PhUGT708A43 is used for C-monoglucosylated flavone production such as vitexin/isovitexin and luteolin-type monoglucosides in pathway reconstruction.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: CGT acts downstream of F2H on 2-hydroxylated intermediates, as shown in Fig. 1 and described in Results.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper explicitly states C-glucosylated intermediates spontaneously dehydrate to Vit/Isovit and Ori/Isoori.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Using PhUGT708A43 in E. coli supported Vit/Isovit production and, with F2H/F3'H modules, Ori/Isoori production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These compounds are flavones/flavonoids in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show the major CGFs are anti-nociceptive and anti-inflammatory, with isoorientin highlighted.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Fig. 2c and Results state leaves store most CGFs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: PhUGT708A43 was heterologously expressed in E. coli and used as the CGT module.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization for PhUGT708A43 is stated in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper includes both leaf tissue accumulation surveys and heterologous fermentation in minimal medium with glucose/tyrosine or naringenin feeding.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described: comparative genomics, cloning, recombinant expression, enzyme assays, LC-MS/MS, HPLC, NMR, kinetics, and heterologous pathway reconstruction.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PhUGT708A43 was combined with PhF2H, F3'H, and AtCPR2 in engineered pathways, but direct protein-protein interaction was not demonstrated.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls CGT key to C-glycoside formation and pathway-specific.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This accurately summarizes the demonstrated in vitro and E. coli production outcomes.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is supported by the Results, including clade A1 status, kinetic efficiency toward 2OHNar, and use in E. coli production.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results and Discussion note substantial accumulation of C-glycosylated 2-hydroxylated intermediates in E. coli, implying incomplete dehydration.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study explicitly frames these genes as useful for metabolic engineering of desired CGFs.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative genomics, phylogenetics, and LC-HR-MS/MS metabolite profiling are all part of the paper.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper uses pET28a/pETDuet and IPTG induction, but does not explicitly name the promoter as T7.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PhUGT708A43 is described as a bamboo CGT ortholog and as the confirmative CGT module in the cell factory.

corrections.reason: No subcellular localization for PhUGT708A43 is stated in the paper.
